observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant recognize the amortization expense and the corresponding accumulated depreciation in determining the carrying value of the Intangible Assets – Computer Software account in accordance with the provisions of IPSAS 31.

We recommended that the Management revise its Municipal Revenue Code in order to strengthen the Municipality’s revenue generating capacity so that more funds will be available for the implementation of the government’s programs, projects and activities in accordance with RA No. 7160.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Discontinue the practice of granting multiple and/or additional cash advances to accountable officers with existing unliquidated cash advances as well as the early recording of the liquidation of cash advances; and
Adhere to Section 49 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, in the process of recording of the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Management:
Conduct proper planning by considering all planned activities for the year and anticipate occurrence of irregular activities to determine the adequate number of bonded personnel and the reasonable amount of fidelity bond to be applied for each accountable officer; and
Consider adopting an Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system for the salaries and wages of municipal employees and job orders to reduce the number and amount of cash advances of the Administrative Officer V/Cashier as well as provide greater convenience, transparency and accountability.

We recommended and the Management agreed to cancel long outstanding checks in the General Fund and Trust Fund and revert the amounts to the unappropriated surplus of the fund and adjust its books for fair presentation of Cash in Bank in the Financial Statements pursuant to the provision of Section 59 of the  Manual for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Implement the projects funded by the fund transfers in order to provide the intended benefits to the public;
Review the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) for the said fund transfers in order to determine if there are special provisions regarding unexpended balances; and
Return the unexpended balances of the fund transfers to the respective source agencies if there are no provisions provided in the MOA that such balances should be retained by the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to submit and observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and
The Municipal Treasurer prepare a report of unclaimed checks for checks that remain unclaimed at the end of each month and submit together with the RCI to facilitate timely submission of documents to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the Management:
Comply with the posting and reporting requirements on the fund utilization and status of program/project implementation of the LGSF-DRRAP in accordance with DBM LBC No. 134 dated January 20, 2021.
Timely implement the project funded by the LGSF-DRRAP in order to deliver the intended government response in addressing the COVID-19 pandemic.
We recommended and the Management agreed to submit the required reports with their supporting documents as prescribed under Sections 1.a.2 and 1.b.1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 20, 2020.

We recommended that the Management maximize utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects and activities and fast-track their implementation following timetables set in the LDRRMF Investment Plan.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipality’s GFPS, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and
The Local Chief Executive ensure inclusion to their succeeding appropriations necessary expenditures, such as manpower requirements, operating expenses and information technology equipment, for the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all PPE at least once a year, submit the RPCPPE that includes all properties and coordinate closely with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of results of the RPCPPE with the accounting records;

The Municipal Accountant reconcile subsidiary ledger cards for all PPEs and account for the lost properties amounting ₱2.247 million in accordance with the policies stipulated in the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and

The General Services Officer – Designate reconcile PCs per category of PPE and indicate in the PCs relevant information for proper monitoring.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer for the preparation of the required reconciliation between actual amounts reflected in the SCBAA and with the amounts presented in the SFP. Likewise, include the said reconciliation in the disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements; and

The Municipal Accountant submit timely the complete set of financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the Perpetual Inventory System for the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials as well as restore the cost of the undistributed/ unconsumed items back to the respective Inventories account at year-end as prescribed by Sections 182, 188 and 190 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and

Direct the General Services Officer – Designate to summarize the RIS, prepare the RSMI monthly and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office to serve as basis in accounting for the issuances of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 189 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to submit and observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the Perpetual Inventory System for the receipt and issuance of inventories such as welfare goods for distribution. Moreover, ensure that all disbursements and liquidations shall be supported with complete documentation; and

Direct the General Services Officer – Designate to prepare and submit the RIS and RSMI to the Municipal Accountant to serve as basis in accounting for the issuances of welfare goods for distribution in accordance with the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Ensure that all disbursements shall be supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and other related issuances; and

Require the concerned PHWs to submit the authority from head of agency/office directing employees to physically report for work during the period of ECQ and documents evidencing actual reporting to the office during the said period in accordance with DBM BC No. 2020-2 dated April 7, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipality:

Maximize utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects and activities and fast-track their implementation following timetables set in the LDRRMF Investment Plan;

Utilize the LDRRMF solely for DRRM-related projects and activities provided in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73; and 

Appropriate a separate budget for the operations of the MDRRMO from where administrative expenditures for personnel services, maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay be charged.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and Municipal Accountant to enforce and monitor compliance with the reporting requirement on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the BRS within 10 days after the end of the month as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-011, to facilitate the timely verification of the balance of the cash in bank accounts of the Municipality and the timely communication and correction of any errors detected.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require all accountable officers to prepare and timely submit the monthly RAAF for proper monitoring in accordance with Section 51 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and

The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Acting Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended that:

The Municipality’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;

The Local Chief Executive ensure inclusion to their succeeding appropriations necessary expenditures, such as manpower requirements, operating expenses and information technology equipment, for the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database; and

The Municipality furnish the Audit Team of the CY 2020 GPB duly reviewed and endorsed by DILG.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Require the municipal officials and employees to attach the statement of account/bill and certification from agency head or his authorized representative for every payment of telephone/ communication expense in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001; and

Ensure that all transactions should be properly recorded in the books of accounts in accordance with GAM for LGUs, Volume III.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant require the municipal officials and employees to support the claims for transportation expenses for taxi and other public utility vehicles with official receipts and/or prepare certification for expenses amounting to ₱300.00 or less in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2017-001.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to various NGOs/POs in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2016-005.

We recommended that Management adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and furnish the Auditor’s Office, copies of contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting documents within the prescribed period of five (5) working days from issuance thereof. However, indicate all the necessary information in the contracts and POs pertaining to the procurement to facilitate review, evaluation process and post audit of transactions, for transparency and for timely and relevant audit results.

We recommended that Management:

Seek assistance from the DA-NMIS in obtaining an accredited contractor and fabricator to be able to complete the project; and

Strictly adhere to the stipulations of the MOA in the implementation of projects under the trust fund.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirements in the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to the concerned barangays in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2016-005.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require strict implementation the provision of COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 particularly on the preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, for audit examination and evaluation.

We recommended that the management immediately enforce the immediate settlement of all audit disallowances exceeding three years as of December 31, 2013 pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.


We also recommended that monthly deduction in the payroll of persons liable be effected to conform with COA policy regarding the settlement of disallowances through installment payment.  The payment period shall be for 36 months only. 


We further recommended that the accounting personnel exert effort to look into the difference between Receivable-Disallowances/charges account reflected in the financial statement and reports issued by Auditor such as RADC.  Henceforth, always reconcile the balance of the said account with RADC and SASDC issued by the auditor quarterly.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all PPE at least once a year, submit the RPCPPE that includes all properties and coordinate closely with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of results of the RPCPPE with the accounting records;
The Municipal Accountant reconcile subsidiary ledger cards for all PPEs and account for the lost properties amounting ₱2.247 million in accordance with the policies stipulated in the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and
The General Services Officer – Designate reconcile PCs per category of PPE and indicate in the PCs relevant information for proper monitoring.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer for the preparation of the required reconciliation between actual amounts reflected in the SCBAA and with the amounts presented in the SFP. Likewise, include the said reconciliation in the disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements; and
The Municipal Accountant submit timely the complete set of financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the Perpetual Inventory System for the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials as well as restore the cost of the undistributed/unconsumed items back to the respective Inventories account at year-end as prescribed by Sections 182, 188 and 190 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and
Direct the General Services Officer – Designate to summarize the RIS, prepare the RSMI monthly and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office to serve as basis in accounting for the issuances of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 189 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to submit and observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the Perpetual Inventory System for the receipt and issuance of inventories such as welfare goods for distribution. Moreover, ensure that all disbursements and liquidations shall be supported with complete documentation; and
Direct the General Services Officer – Designate to prepare and submit the RIS and RSMI to the Municipal Accountant to serve as basis in accounting for the issuances of welfare goods for distribution in accordance with the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Ensure that all disbursements shall be supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and other related issuances; and
Require the concerned PHWs to submit the authority from head of agency/office directing employees to physically report for work during the period of ECQ and documents evidencing actual reporting to the office during the said period in accordance with DBM BC No. 2020-2 dated April 7, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipality:
Maximize utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects and activities and fast-track their implementation following timetables set in the LDRRMF Investment Plan;
Utilize the LDRRMF solely for DRRM-related projects and activities provided in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73; and
Appropriate a separate budget for the operations of the MDRRMO from where administrative expenditures for personnel services, maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay be charged.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and Municipal Accountant to enforce and monitor compliance with the reporting requirement on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant submit the BRS within 10 days after the end of the month as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-011, to facilitate the timely verification of the balance of the cash in bank accounts of the Municipality and the timely communication and correction of any errors detected.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer require all accountable officers to prepare and timely submit the monthly RAAF for proper monitoring in accordance with Section 51 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Acting Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipality’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;
The Local Chief Executive ensure inclusion to their succeeding appropriations necessary expenditures, such as manpower requirements, operating expenses and information technology equipment, for the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database; and
The Municipality furnish the Audit Team of the CY 2020 GPB duly reviewed and endorsed by DILG.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Require the municipal officials and employees to attach the statement of account/bill and certification from agency head or his authorized representative for every payment of telephone/communication expense in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001; and
Ensure that all transactions should be properly recorded in the books of accounts in accordance with GAM for LGUs, Volume III.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant require the municipal officials and employees to support the claims for transportation expenses for taxi and other public utility vehicles with official receipts and/or prepare certification for expenses amounting to ₱300.00 or less in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2017-001.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to various NGOs/POs in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2016-005.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and




The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We recommended that Management adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and furnish the Auditor’s Office, copies of contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting documents within the prescribed period of five (5) working days from issuance thereof. However, indicate all the necessary information in the contracts and POs pertaining to the procurement to facilitate review, evaluation process and post audit of transactions, for transparency and for timely and relevant audit results.

We recommended that Management:

Require the BAC to submit an explanation why the contract was awarded to a non-accredited contractor and fabricator;

Seek assistance from the DA-NMIS in obtaining an accredited contractor and fabricator to be able to complete the project; and


Strictly adhere to the stipulations of the MOA in the implementation of projects under the trust fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer: 

Submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004;

Improve his monitoring and supervision functions and ensure that contractors put up billboards in project sites containing accurate data as soon as the project has been awarded and diligently update the information on the billboard of all projects in order to enhance transparency in project implementation; and

Require the refund of billboard cost on paid projects without billboards or deduct the cost of billboards from amount due to contractors for the projects without billboards.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Engineer:

Conduct sufficient detailed engineering to ensure that specifications of the projects implemented will suit the needs of the recipient barangay and actual dimensions/conditions of the site so that only minimal wastage will be incurred during project implementation;

Ensure that there are engineers/MEO staff to properly supervise the implementation of the projects so that the location of the project and specifications in the POW will be followed as well as the standards on quality workmanship will be met; and


Make sure that materials are completely delivered to the location of the project and that the specifications conform with what is indicated in the purchase order.  The MEO should conduct inspection at the project site and not at the Municipal Hall.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to the concerned barangays in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2016-005.

We recommended that Management exert efforts to implement the solid waste management programs within its targeted timeframe in order to fully attain the objectives of RA No. 9003 on the protection of public health and environment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require strict implementation the provision of COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 particularly on the preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, for audit examination and evaluation.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly for control and monitoring purposes.

We recommended that the management immediately enforce the immediate settlement of all audit disallowances exceeding three years as of December 31, 2013 pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.



We also recommended that monthly deduction in the payroll of persons liable be effected to conform with COA policy regarding the settlement of disallowances through installment payment.  The payment period shall be for 36 months only. 



We further recommended that the accounting personnel exert effort to look into the difference between Receivable-Disallowances/charges account reflected in the financial statement and reports issued by Auditor such as RADC.  Henceforth, always reconcile the balance of the said account with RADC and SASDC issued by the auditor quarterly.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to various NGOs/POs in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2016-005.

The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and
The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We recommended that Management adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and furnish the Auditor’s Office, copies of contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting documents within the prescribed period of five (5) working days from issuance thereof. However, indicate all the necessary information in the contracts and POs pertaining to the procurement to facilitate review, evaluation process and post audit of transactions, for transparency and for timely and relevant audit results.

We recommended that Management:
Require the BAC to submit an explanation why the contract was awarded to a non-accredited contractor and fabricator;
Seek assistance from the DA-NMIS in obtaining an accredited contractor and fabricator to be able to complete the project; and
Strictly adhere to the stipulations of the MOA in the implementation of projects under the trust fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
Submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004;
Improve his monitoring and supervision functions and ensure that contractors put up billboards in project sites containing accurate data as soon as the project has been awarded and diligently update the information on the billboard of all projects in order to enhance transparency in project implementation; and
Require the refund of billboard cost on paid projects without billboards or deduct the cost of billboards from amount due to contractors for the projects without billboards.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Engineer:
Conduct sufficient detailed engineering to ensure that specifications of the projects implemented will suit the needs of the recipient barangay and actual dimensions/conditions of the site so that only minimal wastage will be incurred during project implementation;
Ensure that there are engineers/MEO staff to properly supervise the implementation of the projects so that the location of the project and specifications in the POW will be followed as well as the standards on quality workmanship will be met; and
Make sure that materials are completely delivered to the location of the project and that the specifications conform with what is indicated in the purchase order.  The MEO should conduct inspection at the project site and not at the Municipal Hall.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to the concerned barangays in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2016-005.

We recommended that Management exert efforts to implement the solid waste management programs within its targeted timeframe in order to fully attain the objectives of RA No. 9003 on the protection of public health and environment.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Inventory Committee to:

Conduct annual inventory taking for Property, Plant and Equipment; 

Prepare a Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment using the prescribed form and provide all the data required thereon; and

Instruct the General Services Office and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards and subsidiary ledger for PPE respectively, and ensure that records are reconciled.  

Also, require the Municipal Accountant to establish the book value of unserviceable properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 on the procedures for the write-off of dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers of National Government Agencies (NGAs), Local Government Units (LGUs) and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations (GOCCs).

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to various NGOs/POs in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2016-005 to ensure fair presentation of assets and government equity accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:

The Municipal Treasurer send written notices to the payees or holder in due course to surrender the stale check for cancellation and issuance of a new one;

The Management cancel long outstanding checks and revert the amount to the unappropriated surplus and make the necessary entries to adjust the book balance for fair presentation of Cash in Bank in the financial statements; and

The Municipal Treasurer issue written notices to the payees once their checks are ready for pick-up.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to the concerned barangays in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to ensure fair presentation of assets and government equity accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:

The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and

The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:

Conduct sufficient detailed engineering to ensure that specifications of the projects implemented will suit the needs and actual dimensions/conditions of the site and only minimal wastage will be incurred during project implementation;

Make sure that there are engineers/MEO staff to properly supervise the implementation of the projects so that the location of the project and specifications in the POW will be followed as well as the standards on quality workmanship will be met; and

Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 pertaining to the posting of relevant project information on tarpaulin signboards at the project location.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the original copies of the DVs and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise, provide a legal basis allowing the submission of duplicate/machine copies to the COA.
We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9003 on the operation of controlled dumpsites to ensure the attainment of objectives on ecological solid waste management.
We recommended that the LGU GAD Focal Person prepare and submit the GAD Annual Accomplishment Report in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-02 dated January 12, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require strict implementation the provision of COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 particularly on the preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, for audit examination and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly for control and monitoring purposes. 

Further, we recommended that constant follow up of bank statements be made with the depository bank for prompt preparation of BRS.

We recommended that the management immediately enforce the immediate settlement of all audit disallowances exceeding three years as of December 31, 2013 pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We also recommended that monthly deduction in the payroll of persons liable be effected to conform with COA policy regarding the settlement of disallowances through installment payment.  The payment period shall be for 36 months only. 



We further recommended that the accounting personnel exert effort to look into the difference between Receivable-Disallowances/charges account reflected in the financial statement and reports issued by Auditor such as RADC.  Henceforth, always reconcile the balance of the said account with RADC and SASDC issued by the auditor quarterly.

The municipal accountant should exert efforts to validate and establish the purported investments as recorded in the books of accounts; otherwise seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to write off the said account chargeable against the Government Equity account of the agency.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) take the following courses of action:

The Inventory Committee should conduct physical count of all PPEs and require the preparation and submission of report of physical inventory pursuant to Section 124, Volume 1 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; and

Instruct the General Services Office and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards and subsidiary ledger for PPE respectively, and ensure that records are reconciled.  Also, require the Municipal Accountant to establish the book value of unserviceable properties and request for disposal of the same in accordance with Commission on Audit (COA) Memo No. 92-751. Upon approval of said request, prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable PPE.
observations.reason:
Physical inventory was not conducted and no report was submitted.

Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR Finding No. 1

Finding was first raised in AAR CY 2013 
Finding No. 1

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirements in the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with existing rules and regulations.  Send demand letters to officials and employees requiring liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason:
Substantial amount of cash advances were still not liquidated.  Additional cash advance was also granted to an official with existing unliquidated cash advance.

Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR Finding No. 7

Finding was first raised in AAR CY 2016
Finding No. 2

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the following recommendations:

The Management cancel long outstanding checks and revert the amount to   the unappropriated surplus and make the necessary entries to adjust the book balance for fair presentation of Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable in the financial statements; and

The Municipal Treasurer should issue written notices to the payees once their checks are ready for pick-up.
observations.reason:
Stale checks still remained unadjusted on the books.

Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR Finding No. 8

Finding was first raised in AAR CY 2014
Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require strict implementation the provision of COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 particularly on the preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, for audit examination and evaluation.
observations.reason:
Management was not able to submit the said report.

Finding was first raised in AAR CY 2016 
Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation: The municipal accountant should exert efforts to validate and establish the purported investments as recorded in the books of accounts; otherwise seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to write off the said account chargeable against the Government Equity account of the agency.
observations.reason:
Write-off will be requested to COA

Finding was first raised in AAR CY 2005

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Inventory Committee to:
Conduct annual inventory taking for Property, Plant and Equipment;
Prepare a Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment using the prescribed form and provide all the data required thereon; and
Instruct the General Services Office and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards and subsidiary ledger for PPE respectively, and ensure that records are reconciled.  Also, require the Municipal Accountant to establish the book value of unserviceable properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 on the procedures for the write-off of dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers of National Government Agencies (NGAs), Local Government Units (LGUs) and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations (GOCCs).

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to various NGOs/POs in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2016-005 to ensure fair presentation of assets and government equity accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:
The Municipal Treasurer send written notices to the payees or holder in due course to surrender the stale check for cancellation and issuance of a new one;
The Management cancel long outstanding checks and revert the amount to the unappropriated surplus and make the necessary entries to adjust the book balance for fair presentation of Cash in Bank in the financial statements; and
The Municipal Treasurer issue written notices to the payees once their checks are ready for pick-up.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to the concerned barangays in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to ensure fair presentation of assets and government equity accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:
The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and
The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
Conduct sufficient detailed engineering to ensure that specifications of the projects implemented will suit the needs and actual dimensions/conditions of the site and only minimal wastage will be incurred during project implementation;
Make sure that there are engineers/MEO staff to properly supervise the implementation of the projects so that the location of the project and specifications in the POW will be followed as well as the standards on quality workmanship will be met; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 pertaining to the posting of relevant project information on tarpaulin signboards at the project location.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the original copies of the DVs and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise, provide a legal basis allowing the submission of duplicate/machine copies to the COA.
We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9003 on the operation of controlled dumpsites to ensure the attainment of objectives on ecological solid waste management.
We recommended that the LGU GAD Focal Person prepare and submit the GAD Annual Accomplishment Report in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-02 dated January 12, 2016.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant make necessary adjusting entries based on the RPT Delinquency Report prepared by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to correct the account balances.
observations.reason:
Balances of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer were still not reconciled.

Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR Finding No. 3

Finding was first raised in AAR CY 2015 
Finding No. 4

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU GFPS prepare the GPB in accordance with Paragraph C.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.

We recommended the following:

Management should thoroughly evaluate the proposed projects and see to it that all factors are considered prior to the approval of projects in order to minimize delays and reprogramming of projects;

Management should carefully plan their projects to ensure that what is planned and targeted are achievable and/or implementable during the calendar year; and

Management should strictly monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that projects are completed within the targeted completion date.
observations.reason:
There were still delays in project implementation.

Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR Finding No. 11
Finding was first raised in AAR CY 2017 
Finding No. 4

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to various NGOs/POs in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2016-005 to ensure fair presentation of assets and government equity accounts in the financial statements;

Management strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2007-001 and provide funds only for allowable projects; and

Management cause the refund of the funds transferred to Liga ng mga Barangay for payment of insurance premiums of punong barangays because it was not within the scope of the allowed types of projects under COA Circular No. 2007-001.
observations.reason:
There were still unliquidated financial assistance to NGOs/POs.

Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR Finding No. 6

The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to the concerned barangays in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to ensure fair presentation of assets and government equity accounts in the financial statements.  Granting of fund transfers to barangays with existing unliquidated balance be also refrained.

There were still unliquidated fund transfers to barangays.

Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR Finding No. 9

Finding was first raised in AAR CY 2017 
Finding No. 7

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The LDRRMF should be used solely for DRRM programs, projects and activities as provided for in RA 10121 and its IRR, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG MC No. 2012-73.  Projects similar to those provided by the aforementioned law and circulars may also be funded out of the LDRRMF as long as they pertain to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness and response.

The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer submit a copy of the approved LDRRMFIP to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant review and verify properly, thoroughly and strictly the supporting documents attached for claims against SEF to establish the propriety, legality and conformity of the claim with the pertinent provision of laws, rules and regulations;

The Local School Board to prepare the budget of the SEF in accordance with the provisions of Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160 and other pertinent rules and regulations; and

The original DVs with complete supporting documents be submitted to the Audit Team within the reglementary period.

We recommended the following:

Management should establish a uniform standard on the construction/ establishment of the MRFs that coincide with the guidelines set by RA No. 9003.

Management should designate a specific area and place appropriate signage in the MRFs to promote segregation of waste in the community.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly for control and monitoring purposes. 

Further, we recommended that constant follow up of bank statements be made with the depository bank for prompt preparation of BRS.
observations.reason:
There were still delays in the submission of the BRS.

Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR Finding No. 10

Finding was first raised in AAR CY 2016 
Finding No. 4

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Social Welfare monitor strictly, evaluate and assess the implementation of the livelihood project.  

The DSWD and MSWDO staff provide technical assistance to the association as they venture to sustainable livelihood activities.

We recommended that the management immediately enforce the immediate settlement of all audit disallowances exceeding three years as of December 31, 2013 pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We also recommended that monthly deduction in the payroll of persons liable be effected to conform with COA policy regarding the settlement of disallowances through installment payment.  The payment period shall be for 36 months only. 

We further recommended that the accounting personnel exert effort to look into the difference between Receivable-Disallowances/charges account reflected in the financial statement and reports issued by Auditor such as RADC.  Henceforth, always reconcile the balance of the said account with RADC and SASDC issued by the auditor quarterly.
observations.reason:
Unsettled suspensions and disallowances are being deducted by management from the terminal pay of officers/ employees upon retirement.

Finding was first raised in AAR CY 2013 
Finding No. 4

observations.recommendation: The municipal accountant should exert efforts to validate and establish the purported investments as recorded in the books of accounts; otherwise seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to write off the said account chargeable against the Government Equity account of the agency.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) take the following courses of action:
The Inventory Committee should conduct physical count of all PPEs  and require the preparation and submission of report of physical inventory pursuant to Section 124, Volume 1 of  NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
Instruct the General Services Office and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards and subsidiary ledger for PPE respectively, and ensure that records are reconciled.  Also, require the Municipal Accountant to establish the book value of unserviceable properties and request for disposal of the same in accordance with Commission on Audit (COA) Memo No. 92-751. Upon approval of said request, prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable PPE.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant make necessary adjusting entries based on the RPT Delinquency Report prepared by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to correct the account balances.

We recommended that the LGU GFPS prepare the GPB in accordance with Paragraph C.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.

We recommended the following:
Management should thoroughly evaluate the proposed projects and see to it that all factors are considered prior to the approval of projects in order to minimize delays and reprogramming of projects;
Management should carefully plan their projects to ensure that what is planned and targeted are achievable and/or implementable during the calendar year; and
Management should strictly monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that projects are completed within the targeted completion date.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to various NGOs/POs in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2016-005 to ensure fair presentation of assets and government equity accounts in the financial statements;
Management strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2007-001 and provide funds only for allowable projects; and
Management cause the refund of the funds transferred to Liga ng mga Barangay for payment of insurance premiums of punong barangays because it was not within the scope of the allowed types of projects under COA Circular No. 2007-001.

We reiterated the following recommendations:
The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and
The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to the concerned barangays in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to ensure fair presentation of assets and government equity accounts in the financial statements.  Granting of fund transfers to barangays with existing unliquidated balance be also refrained.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirements in the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with existing rules and regulations.  Send demand letters to officials and employees requiring liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.

We reiterated the following recommendations:
The Management cancel long outstanding checks and revert the amount to   the unappropriated surplus and make the necessary entries to adjust the book balance for fair presentation of Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable in the financial statements; and
The Municipal Treasurer should issue written notices to the payees once their checks are ready for pick-up.

We recommended the following:
The LDRRMF should be used solely for DRRM programs, projects and activities as provided for in RA 10121 and its IRR, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG MC No. 2012-73.  Projects similar to those provided by the aforementioned law and circulars may also be funded out of the LDRRMF as long as they pertain to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness and response.
The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer submit a copy of the approved LDRRMFIP to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant review and verify properly, thoroughly and strictly the supporting documents attached for claims against SEF to establish the propriety, legality and conformity of the claim with the pertinent provision of laws, rules and regulations;
The Local School Board to prepare the budget of the SEF in accordance with the provisions of Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160 and other pertinent rules and regulations; and
The original DVs with complete supporting documents be submitted to the Audit Team within the reglementary period.

We recommended the following:
Management should establish a uniform standard on the construction/ establishment of the MRFs that coincide with the guidelines set by RA No. 9003.
Management should designate a specific area and place appropriate signage in the MRFs to promote segregation of waste in the community.

We recommended that the General Services Office maintain property cards for all types of properties of the LGU, perform physical inventory annually and reconcile balances with the records of the accounting unit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirement in the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with existing regulations. 

Send demand letters to officials and employees requiring liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require strict implementation the provision of COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 particularly on the preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, for audit examination and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly for control and monitoring purposes. 

Further, we recommended that constant follow up of bank statements be made with the depository bank for prompt preparation of BRS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required accounting and financial reports set forth by existing rules and regulations and implement adequate measures that will enable the Agency to meet such deadlines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly enforce and monitor compliance with the reporting requirement on the sources and utilization of MDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal School Board abide strictly with the provisions of Sections 100 and 272 of RA 7160 and related DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars to ensure that SEF is used only for its intended purpose. Expenditures should be limited only to those prioritized under the governing laws, rules and regulations.

We also recommended that original disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents be submitted to the audit team within the reglementary period.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the proper procedures in recording the RPT Receivables. Make necessary adjustments to correct the receivable account.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee make an award of Contract for the supply of goods and services and infrastructure projects undertaken when through small value procurement and other mentioned in Section 54.6 of the IRR of RA 9184 only to suppliers and contractors who have registered with PhilGEPS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive oversee the prompt implementation of the 20% development programs and projects in accordance with the planned schedule to avoid the rising cost of materials and labor and to ensure attainment of the social and economic objectives of these projects.
We recommended that the GAD Focal Person monitor strictly that activities are implemented on the targeted period of implementation so that expected benefits due to beneficiaries will be delivered.

We recommended that the local sanggunian update its existing internal rules of procedures and prescribe performance standards of its members, for purpose of RATA computation in accordance with 7.5 of LBC 103.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Social Welfare monitor strictly, evaluate and assess the implementation of the livelihood project.  

The DSWD and MSWDO staff provide technical assistance to the association as they venture to sustainable livelihood activities.

We recommend that the inventory committee conduct the physical inventory of property and equipment owned by the LGU and prepare RPCPPE to be used as basis for booking up and reconciliation of PPE accounts. 

After reconciliation the Municipal Accountant effect necessary adjustments if any in the books of accounts for fairness of presentation of the PPE account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer of Sibalom, Antique require collecting officers to prepare the Report of Collection and Deposits at the close of each business day and to remit their collections on a daily basis. 

To enforce this policy we recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue an executive order to this effect.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer should conduct joint review of the accounting policies and procedures to strengthen controls with regards to collections and deposits.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to update her Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayers’ Index Cards and submit a certified list of taxpayers with the corresponding amounts to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the calendar year.  

The total amount in the certified list should be reconciled with the total amount per schedule furnished by the Municipal Assessor.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) to liquidate the Confidential/Intelligence Fund cash advance in accordance with the provisions of Joint Circular 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 of the COA, DBM and DND   and the Municipal Accountant to monitor the liquidation of the cash advance to Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit (IFCAU) for audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw the appropriate adjusting journal entries and record the book reconciling items in the respective agency fund books. 

Bank errors should be immediately communicated by the agency to the bank, so that adjustments will be effected by the bank to the bank balances per fund. 

Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer should send written notices to the payees of the existence of the stale checks at least one month before the check become stale to avoid accumulation thereof. 

For long outstanding stale checks or those which have been unclaimed for more than five years, it maybe reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general Fund.

We recommend that the Executive and Legislative Department observe the requirements on Personal Services limitation and should work around only within the limitation to avoid future disallowances on PS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately create a Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all its properties in accordance with the guidelines set forth under Sections 156 and 157 of COA Circular No. 92-386.  

Moreover, the accounting office should exert more efforts to establish records or documents of the PPE to support the existence of these assets, conduct of physical count, prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit the reports to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation and audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of other properties which are not classified under specific PPE accounts like Other Structures, Other Machineries and Equipment, Other Public Infrastructures and Other Assets reflected in the financial statements to support the balances reflected in the financial statements as of December 31, 2013.

We recommended that the management immediately enforce the immediate settlement of all audit disallowances exceeding three years as of December 31, 2013 pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We also recommended that monthly deduction in the payroll of persons liable be effected to conform with COA policy regarding the settlement of disallowances through installment payment.  The payment period shall be for 36 months only. 

We further recommended that the accounting personnel exert effort to look into the difference between Receivable-Disallowances/charges account reflected in the financial statement and reports issued by Auditor such as RADC.  Henceforth, always reconcile the balance of the said account with RADC and SASDC issued by the auditor quarterly.

We recommended that the management require the NGOs/POs to submit the final Fund Utilization Report certified by its Accountant and approved by its President/Chairman to the GO together with the Inspection Report, Certificate of Project Completion, Acceptance Report from beneficiaries for the project, funds, goods, services rendered; and require the Committee in charge to submit the following documents to the Office of the Auditor:

  Certification of registration with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), and/or either the Cooperative Development Authority (CDA) or the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) as the case maybe xxx.  This is to ensure that the NGO/PO has a legal personality; has officers who are responsible and accountable for its operations xxx. Audited Financial Reports for the past three (3) years;
 Complete project proposal approved/signed by its officers which shall include the objectives, target beneficiaries, feasibility studies, risk assessment, designs, plans, blueprints, charts, etc.;
Work and Financial Plan, and Sources and Details of Proponents Equity Participation in the Project.
 List and/or photographs of similar projects previously completed by the NGO/PO, if any, indicating the source of funds for their implementation; and
e) Approved Work and Financial Plan, and Sources and Details of Proponents Equity Participation in the Project.

We also recommended that the Municipality refrain from granting additional funds to NGOs/POs unless the previous release is liquidated and the liquidation documents are post-audited and properly taken in the books.

The municipal accountant should exert efforts to validate and establish the purported investments as recorded in the books of accounts; otherwise seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to write off the said account chargeable against the Government Equity account of the agency.
observations.reason: Seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to write off said account

We recommend that Annual Budget be passed and approved earlier during the year so that projects under the 20% Development Plan can be implemented on the targeted period and expected benefits due the beneficiaries/constituents which  can uplift their living conditions be delivered on time.

We recommend that the personnel concerned be required to immediately liquidate their cash advances. We also recommend strict adherence to the aforementioned provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 to ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that the Honorable Mayor require the Inventory Committee to complete their physical count of the Municipality’s PPE and to prepare and submit the report to the Accountant and Auditor concerned. Thereafter, the reconciliation of the accounting and property records should be undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the:

Management should enforce immediate liquidation of accountabilities by sending a demand letter to all accountable officers including those who are no longer connected with LGU-Sibalom, Antique.

Municipal Accountant should require concerned officials and employees to fully liquidate on time their outstanding cash advances and in case of failure to do so, withhold the salaries of concerned personnel as permitted under Section 16 of Executive Order No. 298 dated March 23, 2004.

2.3. Management refrain from further granting of additional cash advances to officers and employees whose previous cash advance is not liquidated and accounted for in the books as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002.

2.4.Municipal Accountant must see to it that all cash advances, except for petty cash, be fully liquidated at year-end, and any unexpended balance/amount must be refunded to the collecting officer for proper accounting.
observations.reason: Difficulty in collection especially from those who are no longer in service
